import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
from scipy import stats
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency, ttest_ind
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Preprocessing
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer

# Models
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier, VotingClassifier
from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from lightgbm import LGBMClassifier

# Model selection & evaluation
from sklearn.model_selection import (
    train_test_split, StratifiedKFold, cross_val_score
)
from sklearn.metrics import (
    roc_auc_score, roc_curve, classification_report,
    confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay,
    precision_recall_curve, average_precision_score
)

pd.set_option('display.max_columns', 50)
SEED = 42
np.random.seed(SEED)

Data Cleaning¶

In [46]:
# All are uniformly 0 or 100% null — confirmed from describe() output
# Dropped entirely: they carry no information and would confuse the model

zero_signal_cols = [
    "max_dpd_ever_bureau",
    "max_dpd_last_12m",
    "months_since_last_delinquency",
    "has_90plus_dpd_flag",
    "has_30plus_dpd_flag",
    "total_months_delinquent",
]

data.drop(columns=zero_signal_cols, inplace=True)
print(f"After dropping zero-signal bureau payment columns: {data.shape}")

After dropping zero-signal bureau payment columns: (619656, 43)
In [47]:
# loan_id, user_id: identifiers — must never enter the model
# approval_date, due_date: already encoded in tenure; raw dates leak time-specific patterns
# Keep user_id separately for grouped cross-validation splitting before dropping

user_ids   = data["user_id"].copy()   # preserve for StratifiedGroupKFold
loan_ids   = data["loan_id"].copy()   # preserve for prediction output

data.drop(columns=["loan_id", "approval_date", "due_date"], inplace=True)
# user_id dropped after splitting — keep for now
In [48]:
# Values below 18 or above 75 are DOB entry errors in accounts_user

print(f"Age anomalies: {((data['age'] < 18) | (data['age'] > 75)).sum()} rows")

data.loc[(data["age"] < 18) | (data["age"] > 75), "age"] = np.nan

age_median = data["age"].median()
data["age"].fillna(age_median, inplace=True)

print(f"Age after cleaning — min: {data['age'].min()}, max: {data['age'].max()}, nulls: {data['age'].isna().sum()}")

Age anomalies: 338 rows
Age after cleaning — min: 18.0, max: 75.0, nulls: 0
In [49]:
# Max plausible credit history: ~50 years = 600 months

print(f"credit_history_months > 300: {(data['credit_history_months'] > 300).sum()} rows")

data.loc[data["credit_history_months"] > 300, "credit_history_months"] = 300

data["credit_history_months_missing"] = data["credit_history_months"].isna().astype(int)
data["credit_history_months"].fillna(0, inplace=True)

credit_history_months > 300: 9753 rows
In [50]:
# 99999 is FirstCentral's sentinel for unrated accounts — not a real score

print(f"bureau_account_rating == 99999: {(data['bureau_account_rating'] == 99999).sum()} rows")

data.loc[data["bureau_account_rating"] == 99999, "bureau_account_rating"] = np.nan

median_rating_fc = data.loc[
    data["bureau_availability"] == "firstcentral", "bureau_account_rating"
].median()

data.loc[
    (data["bureau_account_rating"].isna()) &
    (data["bureau_availability"] == "firstcentral"),
    "bureau_account_rating"
] = median_rating_fc

data.loc[
    (data["bureau_account_rating"].isna()) &
    (data["bureau_availability"].isin(["no_bureau", "thin_file"])),
    "bureau_account_rating"
] = 0

print(f"bureau_account_rating nulls remaining: {data['bureau_account_rating'].isna().sum()}")

bureau_account_rating == 99999: 44 rows
bureau_account_rating nulls remaining: 0
In [51]:
# Bureau columns split into two categories:
# (A) Count/amount fields → impute with 0 (no bureau = no recorded accounts/debt)
# (B) Ratio/derived fields → impute with 0 but also flag missingness

# Category A: zero-impute (no bureau record means no recorded exposure)
bureau_zero_impute = [
    "total_bureau_accounts",
    "accounts_in_arrears",
    "accounts_bad_condition",
    "accounts_good_condition",
    "amount_in_arrears",
    "total_outstanding_debt",
    "total_monthly_obligations",
    "has_written_off_account",
    "count_written_off_accounts",
    "has_doubtful_account",
    "distinct_lender_count",
    "total_enquiry_count",
    "distinct_enquiring_lenders",
]

for col in bureau_zero_impute:
    data[col].fillna(0, inplace=True)

# Category B: ratio/derived — impute with 0 but flag
# These are undefined for no-bureau users, so the flag is informative
bureau_ratio_cols = [
    "prop_bad",
    "bureau_dsti",
    "bureau_annual_dti",
]

for col in bureau_ratio_cols:
    data[f"{col}_missing"] = data[col].isna().astype(int)
    data[col].fillna(0, inplace=True)

print("Bureau imputation complete.")
print(data[bureau_zero_impute + bureau_ratio_cols].isna().sum())

# 15.64% missing on acquisition_channel is structural, not random
# Correct approach: treat as its own category — the model can learn its risk profile

data["acquisition_channel"].fillna("Unknown", inplace=True)

print(data["acquisition_channel"].value_counts())

In [53]:
# Missingness < 0.02% — negligible. Standard imputation appropriate.

# Categorical: mode imputation
for col in ["state", "marital_status", "gender"]:
    mode_val = data[col].mode()[0]
    data[col].fillna(mode_val, inplace=True)
    print(f"{col}: filled {data[col].isna().sum()} remaining nulls with mode='{mode_val}'")

# salary: median imputation (income is right-skewed — median is more robust than mean)
salary_median = data["salary"].median()
data["salary"].fillna(salary_median, inplace=True)
print(f"salary: filled with median={salary_median:,.0f}")

state: filled 0 remaining nulls with mode='Lagos'
marital_status: filled 0 remaining nulls with mode='Married'
gender: filled 0 remaining nulls with mode='male'
salary: filled with median=100,000
In [54]:
# 0.69% missing — impute with mode (Tertiary)

data["edu_status_missing"] = data["edu_status"].isna().astype(int)
data["edu_status"].fillna(data["edu_status"].mode()[0], inplace=True)

# 1 missing row — cardinal group cannot be reliably imputed
# Drop the row: loss of 1/619,656 = 0.0002% of data
data.dropna(subset=["global_cardinal"], inplace=True)
print(f"After dropping null cardinal: {data.shape}")

After dropping null cardinal: (619655, 45)
In [56]:
# Cast all winsorise target columns to float64 first
# Decimal type comes from DuckDB NUMERIC/DECIMAL storage preserved on CSV export
# pandas quantile() cannot interpolate between Decimal values — requires native float

winsorise_cols = {
    "salary":                    0.99,
    "total_bureau_accounts":     0.99,
    "accounts_in_arrears":       0.99,
    "accounts_bad_condition":    0.99,
    "accounts_good_condition":   0.99,
    "amount_in_arrears":         0.99,
    "total_outstanding_debt":    0.99,
    "total_monthly_obligations": 0.99,
    "distinct_lender_count":     0.99,
    "total_enquiry_count":       0.99,
    "loan_to_income_ratio":      0.99,
    "bureau_dsti":               0.99,
    "bureau_annual_dti":         0.99,
    "principal_due":             0.99,
    "credit_limit":              0.99,
}

# Cast to float64 before winsorising
for col in winsorise_cols:
    data[col] = data[col].astype("float64")

# Now winsorise safely
for col, pct in winsorise_cols.items():
    cap       = data[col].quantile(pct)
    n_capped  = (data[col] > cap).sum()
    data[col] = data[col].clip(upper=cap)
    print(f"{col}: capped {n_capped:,} rows at {cap:,.2f} (p{int(pct*100)})")

from decimal import Decimal

decimal_cols = [
    col for col in data.columns
    if data[col].dropna().apply(
        lambda x: isinstance(x, Decimal)
    ).any()
]

if decimal_cols:
    print(f"Casting {len(decimal_cols)} Decimal columns to float64: {decimal_cols}")
    data[decimal_cols] = data[decimal_cols].astype("float64")
else:
    print("No remaining Decimal columns detected.")

PALETTE     = {"0": "#2E86C1", "1": "#E74C3C"}
DEFAULT_CLR = "#E74C3C"
SAFE_CLR    = "#2E86C1"
NEUTRAL_CLR = "#2C3E50"

df_eda = data.copy()
In [60]:
def default_rate_summary(df, col, top_n=None):
    """Return default rate table for a categorical column."""
    tbl = (
        df.groupby(col)["target"]
        .agg(count="count", defaults="sum")
        .assign(default_rate=lambda x: x["defaults"] / x["count"])
        .assign(pct_of_total=lambda x: x["count"] / len(df))
        .sort_values("default_rate", ascending=False)
        .reset_index()
    )
    if top_n:
        tbl = tbl.head(top_n)
    return tbl


def plot_default_rate_bar(df, col, title=None, top_n=None,
                           rotate=False, figsize=(10, 4)):
    """Bar chart: count (left y) + default rate line (right y)."""
    tbl = default_rate_summary(df, col, top_n=top_n)
    fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)
    ax2 = ax1.twinx()

    bars = ax1.bar(tbl[col].astype(str), tbl["count"],
                   color=SAFE_CLR, alpha=0.7, label="Loan Count")
    ax2.plot(tbl[col].astype(str), tbl["default_rate"],
             color=DEFAULT_CLR, marker="o", linewidth=2,
             markersize=6, label="Default Rate")

    ax1.set_ylabel("Loan Count", color=SAFE_CLR)
    ax2.set_ylabel("Default Rate", color=DEFAULT_CLR)
    ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
    ax2.set_ylim(0, tbl["default_rate"].max() * 1.4)

    if rotate:
        ax1.set_xticklabels(tbl[col].astype(str), rotation=45, ha="right")

    ax1.set_title(title or f"Default Rate by {col}")
    fig.legend(loc="upper right", bbox_to_anchor=(1, 1),
               bbox_transform=ax1.transAxes)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    print(tbl.to_string(index=False,
          float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.2%}"
          if isinstance(x, float) and x <= 1 else f"{x:,.0f}"))
    return tbl


def plot_numeric_univariate(df, col, bins=50, figsize=(12, 4)):
    """Distribution + boxplot for a numeric column."""
    # Cast to float64 defensively — prevents Decimal type errors
    # from PostgreSQL NUMERIC columns exported via Metabase CSV
    tmp = df[[col, "target"]].copy()
    tmp[col] = tmp[col].astype("float64")
    series = tmp[col].dropna()

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=figsize)

    axes[0].hist(series, bins=bins, color=SAFE_CLR,
                 alpha=0.8, edgecolor="white")
    axes[0].axvline(series.median(), color=DEFAULT_CLR,
                    linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5,
                    label=f"Median: {series.median():,.1f}")
    axes[0].axvline(series.mean(), color=NEUTRAL_CLR,
                    linestyle=":", linewidth=1.5,
                    label=f"Mean: {series.mean():,.1f}")
    axes[0].set_title(f"{col} — Distribution")
    axes[0].legend(fontsize=8)

    tmp.dropna().boxplot(
        column=col, by="target", ax=axes[1],
        boxprops=dict(color=SAFE_CLR),
        medianprops=dict(color=DEFAULT_CLR, linewidth=2),
        flierprops=dict(marker=".", alpha=0.2, markersize=2)
    )
    axes[1].set_title(f"{col} — By Default Status")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Target (0=Paid, 1=Default)")
    plt.suptitle("")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    print(f"\n{col} summary:")
    print(series.describe().apply(lambda x: f"{x:,.2f}").to_string())


def plot_numeric_default_deciles(df, col, n_deciles=10, figsize=(10, 4)):
    """Default rate by decile — monotonicity check for model features."""
    tmp = df[[col, "target"]].copy()
    tmp[col] = tmp[col].astype("float64")
    tmp = tmp.dropna()

    try:
        tmp["decile"] = pd.qcut(tmp[col], q=n_deciles,
                                duplicates="drop", labels=False)
    except ValueError:
        print(f"  ⚠ {col}: too many ties for decile split — skipping")
        return

    tbl = (tmp.groupby("decile")["target"]
           .agg(count="count", default_rate="mean")
           .reset_index())

    fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)
    ax2 = ax1.twinx()

    ax1.bar(tbl["decile"], tbl["count"], color=SAFE_CLR, alpha=0.6)
    ax2.plot(tbl["decile"], tbl["default_rate"],
             color=DEFAULT_CLR, marker="o", linewidth=2)

    ax1.set_xlabel(f"{col} Decile (0=lowest, {len(tbl)-1}=highest)")
    ax1.set_ylabel("Count", color=SAFE_CLR)
    ax2.set_ylabel("Default Rate", color=DEFAULT_CLR)
    ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
    ax1.set_title(f"{col} — Default Rate by Decile (Monotonicity Check)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

def cramers_v(x, y):
    """Cramér's V — association between two categoricals."""
    ct  = pd.crosstab(x, y)
    chi2 = stats.chi2_contingency(ct)[0]
    n    = len(x)
    k    = min(ct.shape) - 1
    return np.sqrt(chi2 / (n * k)) if k > 0 else 0
Target Distribution¶
In [61]:
target_counts = df_eda["target"].value_counts()
target_pct    = df_eda["target"].value_counts(normalize=True)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

# Count bar
axes[0].bar(["Non-Default (0)", "Default (1)"],
            target_counts.values,
            color=[SAFE_CLR, DEFAULT_CLR], alpha=0.85)
for i, v in enumerate(target_counts.values):
    axes[0].text(i, v + 2000, f"{v:,}", ha="center",
                 fontweight="bold", fontsize=10)
axes[0].set_title("Loan Count by Target")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Count")

# Pie
axes[1].pie(target_counts.values,
            labels=[f"Non-Default\n{target_pct[0]:.1%}",
                    f"Default\n{target_pct[1]:.1%}"],
            colors=[SAFE_CLR, DEFAULT_CLR],
            autopct="%1.1f%%", startangle=90,
            wedgeprops={"edgecolor": "white", "linewidth": 2})
axes[1].set_title("Class Balance")

plt.suptitle("Target Variable Distribution", fontweight="bold", fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\nTotal loans:     {len(df_eda):,}")
print(f"Non-defaults:    {target_counts[0]:,}  ({target_pct[0]:.2%})")
print(f"Defaults:        {target_counts[1]:,}  ({target_pct[1]:.2%})")
print(f"\nClass imbalance ratio (neg:pos): {target_counts[0]/target_counts[1]:.1f}:1")

Total loans:     619,655
Non-defaults:    490,830  (79.21%)
Defaults:        128,825  (20.79%)

Class imbalance ratio (neg:pos): 3.8:1

print("\n── Bureau Availability ──")
plot_default_rate_bar(df_eda, "bureau_availability", title="Default Rate by Bureau Availability Population")

print("\n── Bureau Risk Category ──")
plot_default_rate_bar(df_eda, "bureau_risk_category", title="Default Rate by Bureau Risk Category")

print("\n── Thin File Flag ──")
tbl_thin = default_rate_summary(df_eda.assign(
    thin_file=df_eda["is_thin_file"].map({0: "No", 1: "Yes (Thin File)"})),
    "thin_file")
print(tbl_thin.to_string(index=False))

print("\n── Bureau Health Score ──")
plot_default_rate_bar(df_eda, "bureau_health_score", title="Default Rate by Bureau Health Score (0=No Bureau, 3=Best)")

print("\n── Bureau Account Counts (FirstCentral users only) ──")
fc_only = df_eda[df_eda["bureau_availability"] == "firstcentral"].copy()

for col in ["total_bureau_accounts", "accounts_bad_condition",
            "accounts_in_arrears", "accounts_good_condition"]:
    print(f"\n  {col}:")
    plot_numeric_univariate(fc_only, col, bins=40, figsize=(12, 3))
    plot_numeric_default_deciles(fc_only, col)

print("\n── Debt Burden Features (FirstCentral users only) ──")
for col in ["total_outstanding_debt", "amount_in_arrears",
            "total_monthly_obligations"]:
    print(f"\n  {col}:")
    plot_numeric_default_deciles(fc_only, col)

print("\n── Debt Service & Leverage Ratios (FirstCentral users only) ──")
for col in ["bureau_dsti", "bureau_annual_dti"]:
    print(f"\n  {col}:")
    plot_numeric_univariate(fc_only, col, bins=40, figsize=(12, 3))
    plot_numeric_default_deciles(fc_only, col)

print("\n── Delinquency Flags ──")
flag_cols = ["has_written_off_account", "has_doubtful_account", "is_thin_file"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4))
for i, col in enumerate(flag_cols):
    tbl = default_rate_summary(
        df_eda.assign(**{col: df_eda[col].map({0: "No", 1: "Yes"})}), col)
    axes[i].bar(tbl[col], tbl["default_rate"],
                color=[SAFE_CLR, DEFAULT_CLR], alpha=0.85)
    axes[i].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
    axes[i].set_title(col.replace("_", " ").title())
    axes[i].set_ylabel("Default Rate")
    for j, row in tbl.iterrows():
        axes[i].text(j, row["default_rate"] + 0.005,
                     f"{row['default_rate']:.1%}\nn={row['count']:,}",
                     ha="center", fontsize=8.5)

plt.suptitle("Default Rate by Delinquency Flags", fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\n── Distinct Lender Count (Credit Stacking) ──")
plot_default_rate_bar(
    df_eda.assign(lender_band=pd.cut(df_eda["distinct_lender_count"],
                  bins=[-1, 0, 1, 2, 5, 100],
                  labels=["0 (No Bureau)", "1", "2", "3-5", "6+"])),
    "lender_band",
    title="Default Rate by Number of Distinct Lenders (Credit Stacking Signal)"
)

print("\n── Credit History Length (FC users only) ──")
plot_numeric_univariate(fc_only, "credit_history_months", figsize=(12, 3))
plot_numeric_default_deciles(fc_only, "credit_history_months")

print("\n── Proportion of Accounts in Bad Condition (FC users only) ──")
plot_numeric_default_deciles(fc_only, "prop_bad")

print("\n── Credit Enquiry Count ──")
plot_default_rate_bar(
    df_eda.assign(enq_band=pd.cut(df_eda["total_enquiry_count"],
                  bins=[-1, 0, 1, 2, 5, 100],
                  labels=["0", "1", "2", "3-5", "6+"])),
    "enq_band",
    title="Default Rate by Total Credit Enquiry Count"
)

In [65]:
print("\n── Loans Per User Distribution ──")
loans_per_user = df_eda.groupby("user_id").size()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.hist(loans_per_user, bins=50, color=SAFE_CLR, alpha=0.8, edgecolor="white")
ax.axvline(loans_per_user.median(), color=DEFAULT_CLR, linestyle="--",
           linewidth=1.5, label=f"Median: {loans_per_user.median():.0f}")
ax.set_title("Distribution of Loans per User")
ax.set_xlabel("Number of Loans")
ax.set_ylabel("Number of Users")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\nLoans per user:")
print(f"  Median:  {loans_per_user.median():.0f}")
print(f"  Mean:    {loans_per_user.mean():.1f}")
print(f"  Max:     {loans_per_user.max():.0f}")
print(f"  Users with >20 loans: {(loans_per_user > 20).sum():,}")
print(f"  Users with >50 loans: {(loans_per_user > 50).sum():,}")

# Default rate by loan count band — high count users may be different risk
user_loan_counts = loans_per_user.reset_index()
user_loan_counts.columns = ["user_id", "loan_count"]
tmp = df_eda.merge(user_loan_counts, on="user_id")
tmp["loan_count_band"] = pd.cut(tmp["loan_count"],
    bins=[0, 1, 3, 5, 10, 20, 9999],
    labels=["1", "2-3", "4-5", "6-10", "11-20", "21+"])
plot_default_rate_bar(tmp, "loan_count_band",
    title="Default Rate by Total Loans per User (Frequency Check)")

print("\n── Salary Anomalies ──")
salary_zero   = (df_eda["salary"] == 0).sum()
salary_p99    = df_eda["salary"].quantile(0.99)
salary_median = df_eda["salary"].median()

print(f"  Salary = 0:  {salary_zero:,} rows ({salary_zero/len(df_eda):.2%})")
print(f"  Salary median:  ₦{salary_median:,.0f}")
print(f"  Salary p99:  ₦{salary_p99:,.0f}")

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 3))
ax.hist(df_eda[df_eda["salary"] > 0]["salary"],
        bins=60, color=SAFE_CLR, alpha=0.8, edgecolor="white")
ax.axvline(salary_median, color=DEFAULT_CLR, linestyle="--",
           label=f"Median: ₦{salary_median:,.0f}")
ax.set_title("Salary Distribution (Zero values excluded)")
ax.set_xlabel("Salary (₦)")
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(
    lambda x, _: f"₦{x/1000:.0f}k"))
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Default rate for zero-salary vs non-zero
tbl_salary = default_rate_summary(
    df_eda.assign(salary_band=df_eda["salary"].apply(
        lambda x: "Zero" if x == 0 else ("≤50k" if x <= 50000
        else ("51k-200k" if x <= 200000
        else ("201k-500k" if x <= 500000 else "500k+"))))),
    "salary_band")
print("\nDefault rate by salary band:")
print(tbl_salary.to_string(index=False))

print("\n── Bureau Account Rating Distribution ──")
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 3))
# Exclude 0 (imputed no-bureau) to see the real FC distribution
fc_rating = fc_only["bureau_account_rating"]
fc_rating  = fc_rating[fc_rating > 0]
ax.hist(fc_rating, bins=60, color=SAFE_CLR, alpha=0.8, edgecolor="white")
ax.set_title("Bureau Account Rating — FC Users with Rating > 0")
ax.set_xlabel("Rating")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\n  Rating = 0 (imputed/thin):  {(df_eda['bureau_account_rating'] == 0).sum():,}")
print(f"  Rating > 0 (real score):    {(df_eda['bureau_account_rating'] > 0).sum():,}")
print(f"  Median (non-zero):          {fc_rating.median():.0f}")
print(f"  Max (after sentinel fix):   {df_eda['bureau_account_rating'].max():.0f}")

print("\n── Limit Utilisation Ratio — Spike at 1.0 ──")
util_exact_1 = (df_eda["limit_util_ratio"] == 1.0).sum()
print(f"  Loans with util_ratio = exactly 1.0: {util_exact_1:,} ({util_exact_1/len(df_eda):.1%})")
print(f"  This means principal_due == credit_limit for these loans")
print(f"  Not an error — Quickcheck often sets limit = requested amount")

dr_util_1  = df_eda[df_eda["limit_util_ratio"] == 1.0]["target"].mean()
dr_util_lt1 = df_eda[df_eda["limit_util_ratio"] < 1.0]["target"].mean()
print(f"\n  Default rate, util = 1.0:  {dr_util_1:.2%}")
print(f"  Default rate, util < 1.0:  {dr_util_lt1:.2%}")

print("\n── Feature Correlation Matrix ──")
numeric_cols = [
    "global_cardinal", "principal_due", "credit_limit", "limit_util_ratio",
    "tenure", "age", "salary", "loan_to_income_ratio",
    "total_bureau_accounts", "accounts_in_arrears", "accounts_bad_condition",
    "accounts_good_condition", "amount_in_arrears", "total_outstanding_debt",
    "total_monthly_obligations", "prop_bad", "bureau_dsti", "bureau_annual_dti",
    "has_written_off_account", "has_doubtful_account", "is_thin_file",
    "distinct_lender_count", "credit_history_months", "bureau_health_score",
    "total_enquiry_count", "bureau_account_rating",
]

corr = df_eda[numeric_cols + ["target"]].corr()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 13))
mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, dtype=bool))
sns.heatmap(corr, mask=mask, annot=False, fmt=".2f",
            cmap="RdBu_r", center=0, vmin=-1, vmax=1,
            linewidths=0.3, ax=ax,
            cbar_kws={"shrink": 0.6})
ax.set_title("Feature Correlation Matrix\n(Lower triangle — includes target)",
             fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\nHigh-correlation feature pairs (|r| > 0.85) — review before modeling:")
corr_pairs = (corr.where(np.tril(np.ones(corr.shape), k=-1).astype(bool))
              .stack().reset_index())
corr_pairs.columns = ["feature_1", "feature_2", "correlation"]
high_corr = corr_pairs[corr_pairs["correlation"].abs() > 0.85].sort_values(
    "correlation", key=abs, ascending=False)

if len(high_corr):
    print(high_corr.to_string(index=False))
else:
    print("  No pairs above 0.85 threshold.")

print("\n── Feature Correlation with Target (Ranking) ──")
target_corr = (corr["target"]
               .drop("target")
               .abs()
               .sort_values(ascending=False)
               .reset_index())
target_corr.columns = ["feature", "abs_correlation_with_target"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 10))
colors_bar = [DEFAULT_CLR if x > 0.05 else "#BDC3C7"
              for x in target_corr["abs_correlation_with_target"]]
ax.barh(target_corr["feature"], target_corr["abs_correlation_with_target"],
        color=colors_bar, alpha=0.85)
ax.axvline(0.05, color=NEUTRAL_CLR, linestyle="--",
           linewidth=1, label="|r| = 0.05 threshold")
ax.set_xlabel("|Pearson r| with Target")
ax.set_title("Feature Correlation Ranking with Target\n(Red = |r| > 0.05)")
ax.legend()
ax.invert_yaxis()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\nTop 10 features by absolute correlation with target:")
print(target_corr.head(10).to_string(index=False))
print("\nBottom 5 features by absolute correlation with target:")
print(target_corr.tail(5).to_string(index=False))

EDA Summary¶
In [66]:
overall_dr = df_eda["target"].mean()
c1_dr      = df_eda[df_eda["cardinal_group"] == "C1"]["target"].mean()
c25_dr     = df_eda[df_eda["cardinal_group"] == "C25+"]["target"].mean()
no_bur_dr  = df_eda[df_eda["bureau_availability"] == "no_bureau"]["target"].mean()
fc_dr      = df_eda[df_eda["bureau_availability"] == "firstcentral"]["target"].mean()
wo_dr      = df_eda[df_eda["has_written_off_account"] == 1]["target"].mean()
thin_dr    = df_eda[df_eda["is_thin_file"] == 1]["target"].mean()
doubtful_dr = df_eda[df_eda["has_doubtful_account"] == 1]["target"].mean()

summary = {
    "Overall default rate":               f"{overall_dr:.2%}",
    "C1 default rate":                    f"{c1_dr:.2%}",
    "C25+ default rate":                  f"{c25_dr:.2%}",
    "No-bureau default rate":             f"{no_bur_dr:.2%}",
    "FirstCentral user default rate":     f"{fc_dr:.2%}",
    "Has written-off account (DR)":       f"{wo_dr:.2%}",
    "Thin file default rate":             f"{thin_dr:.2%}",
    "Has doubtful account (DR)":          f"{doubtful_dr:.2%}",
    "Class imbalance ratio":
        f"{(df_eda['target']==0).sum()/(df_eda['target']==1).sum():.1f}:1",
    "Recommended scale_pos_weight":
        f"{(df_eda['target']==0).sum()/(df_eda['target']==1).sum():.2f}",
    "Payment history columns":            "DROPPED — confirmed SQL index bug",
    "bureau_health_score max observed":   f"{df_eda['bureau_health_score'].max():.0f} (expected 5 — FC has no score)",
}

for k, v in summary.items():
    print(f"  {k:<42} {v}")

  Overall default rate                       20.79%
  C1 default rate                            42.93%
  C25+ default rate                          5.38%
  No-bureau default rate                     16.55%
  FirstCentral user default rate             20.69%
  Has written-off account (DR)               25.37%
  Thin file default rate                     26.04%
  Has doubtful account (DR)                  22.64%
  Class imbalance ratio                      3.8:1
  Recommended scale_pos_weight               3.81
  Payment history columns                    DROPPED — confirmed SQL index bug
  bureau_health_score max observed           3 (expected 5 — FC has no score)
